rulings.Case Title: GATEWAY BUSINESS BANK, A CALIFORNIA COLLECTION et al VS. VILLA DEI FIORI CONDOMINIUMS L.P. A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 9, 2009, line 3, PLAINTIFF GATEWAY BUSINESS BANK, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part, and denied in part. Summary adjudication granted as to the first cause of action for breach of contract (promissory note). Cross-defendant shifted the burden and cross-complainants fail to raise a triable issue of fact. The loan agreement provides that the lender has no obligation to make advances, if the borrower is in default (UMF #17), and cross-complainant was in default (UMF#3). No notice of Default is required. Motion for summary adjudication granted as to second cause of action for breach of contract (modification). Cross-defendant shifted the burden and cross-complainants fail to raise a triable issue of fact. No eivdence that any purported oral modification was memoralized in writing. Motion denied as to remaining causes of action. A conclusory statement of the law does not suffice to show that cross-complainants' evidence is factually devoid. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN C MCKEAN et al VS. STEPHEN E LAWRENCE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motionto Disqualify Plaintiffs' Counsel And Exclude Evidence For Violations Of California Rules Of Professional Conduct Rule 2-100; Request For Judicial Notice, And Declarations Of Russ Martin And Stephen Lawrence In Support
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 9, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT STEPHEN LAWRENCE, and, SOPHIE GASPARATOS' Motion to Disqualify Plaintiffs' Counsel and Exclude Evidence For Violations Of California Rules Of Professional Conduct Rule 2-100. Motion to disqualify plaintiff's counsel and exclude evidence is denied. Request for Judicial Notice is denied. Unofficial transcript excluded as hearsay. Although counsel may have violated CRPC 2-100, the mediator at the Rent Board Hearing specifically inquired about Lawrence's representation by counsel. Lawrence chose to represent himself at the Rent Board Hearing. The, on the record communication, at the hearing may be authorized by Rule 2-100(c)(3). Plaintiffs would be severly disadvantaged if counsel is disqualified. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE YE VS. REALOGY CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer To Cross-Complaint; Demurrer; Request For Judicial Notice Filed In Joint Support Of Demurrer To Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 9, 2009, line 7, PLAINTIFF MICHELLE YE'S Demurrer To Cross-Complaint. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to Count One, Harassment. Cross-complainant cannot allege in good faith that he complied with the FEHA administrative process. Sustained without leave to amend as to Count Nine, Negligent Interference with Contractual Relations. (See Fifield Manor vs. Finston (1960) 54 Cal.2nd 632. No reasonable probability that defect can be curred by amendment. Demurrer is overruled as to stalking, libel, and slander causes of action. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MICHELLE YE VS. REALOGY CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice Filed Joint Support Of Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 9, 2009, line 7, PLAINTIFF MICHELLE YE'S Motion To Strike Cross-Complaint. Motion to strike is granted without leave to amend all cited allegations except for page 6, lines 15-20, which are germane to the Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress and Stalking causes of action. The stricken allegations are irrelevant and improper within the meaning of CCP section 436(a). =(302/CWW/PB)
